headline: Agility Robotics rotation-backfill cycle (since May 7 first-agent baseline). Canonical composite 60.9 / established band sustained. Compound-positive baseline holds: Toyota Motor Manufacturing Canada Feb 19 2026 commercial agreement (7 Digit robots at Woodstock, Ontario, RAV4 logistics RaaS contract); 2022 anti-weaponization pledge signatory status sustained; ASTM humanoid safety standards working group participation continuing — May 27-28 2026 Boston convening upcoming; GXO and Spanx pre-existing deployments without weaponization/surveillance record. No new material score-moving evidence this cycle. Recommend confirm at 60.9 / established. Math reconstruction clean (60.9 published = 60.9 reconstructed). No boundary case (60.9 sits 19.9 points inside established band).

scoring_rationale.AWR: 3.5 confirmed. A1 detection: structured pilot-to-commercial process surfaces operational issues; A4 amplification: ASTM standards-working-group participation sustains cross-vendor safety signals.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 3.5 confirmed. E1 affective resonance: 'cooperatively safe humanoid' framing carries forward.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 3.5 confirmed. AC2 proportionality: Toyota year-long evaluation pilot precedent established structured deployment process; AC4 response framework integrated.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 3.0 confirmed. EQ2 distributional awareness: RaaS contract structure remains a sub-threshold labor-displacement equity question; no documented displacement events in current window.
scoring_rationale.BND: 3.5 confirmed. B3 scope clarity: anti-weaponization pledge sustained; B5 consent: factory-logistics deployment context aligns with declared use cases.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 3.5 confirmed. AB2 transparency: commercial-agreement and pilot-process disclosure functional; AB4 reparation: RaaS structure provides commercial-level accountability.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 3.5 confirmed. S1 causal reasoning: ASTM standards-working-group participation indicates industry-level systemic-harm reasoning; ASTM May 27-28 Boston convening upcoming.
scoring_rationale.INT: 3.5 confirmed. I1 consistency: 2022 anti-weaponization pledge maintained through 2026 commercial deployment; declared safety-first commitment consistent with operational pattern.

key_evidence: Carry-over anchor: Feb 19 2026 Toyota Motor Manufacturing Canada commercial agreement — 7 Digit humanoids at Woodstock, Ontario, RAV4 logistics RaaS contract.
Carry-over: year-long evaluation pilot (development → proof-of-technology → live onsite) preceded commercial conversion.
Carry-over: 2022 anti-weaponization pledge signatory (alongside Boston Dynamics, ANYbotics, Clearpath, Open Robotics, Unitree).
Upcoming: ASTM humanoid safety standards convening May 27-28 2026 Boston — to be watched in next cycles.
Carry-over: 'cooperatively safe humanoid robot to work alongside people' public commitment.
Carry-over: GXO and Spanx Inc. pre-existing deployments without weaponization/surveillance record.
No new material score-moving evidence in current cycle (May 7-13 window).

evidence.source: Agility Robotics — Commercial Agreement with Toyota Motor Manufacturing Canada (anchor)
evidence.finding: First commercial humanoid deployment in Canadian automotive industry; year-long evaluation pilot; cooperatively-safe-humanoid framing.

evidence.source: The Robot Report — Toyota Motor Manufacturing Canada to deploy Agility Robotics' Digit humanoids (anchor)
evidence.finding: RaaS contract structure; 7 Digit units active; RAV4 logistics application.

evidence.source: The Robot Report — Agility, Boston Dynamics, ASTM to discuss state of humanoid robotics (carry-over)
evidence.finding: ASTM humanoid safety standards working group participation; May 27-28 Boston convening upcoming.

evidence.source: Fortune — 6 major robot makers anti-weaponization pledge (anchor)
evidence.finding: Agility 2022 anti-weaponization pledge signatory; sustained through 2026.

math_check.validator_check: Reconstruction confirms 60.9. Established band sustained. No discrepancy. Confirm at 60.9.

negativeSignals: RaaS contract structure raises sub-threshold labor-displacement equity question; humanoid commercial-deployment pre-dates EU Machinery Regulation (2027) and OSHA-humanoid guidance (2027) — regulatory-gap structural risk; no published independent third-party safety audit; pricing/contract terms not fully transparent.
positiveSignals: Toyota Canada Feb 2026 commercial deployment with structured year-long evaluation pilot; 2022 anti-weaponization pledge signatory; ASTM humanoid safety standards working group leadership (May 27-28 Boston convening upcoming); 'cooperatively safe humanoid' framing; GXO and Spanx pre-existing deployments without weaponization/surveillance record.
sector_context: Robotics Labs / commercial-humanoid cluster. Rotation-backfill closure. Cross-reference: Apptronik (Mercedes/GXO/Jabil pilots, no pledge, math-hygiene +8.0 flag), Boston Dynamics (pledge co-signatory, Hyundai deployment), Figure AI (BMW pilot), 1X Technologies (NEO consumer), Tesla Optimus (no pledge). Agility distinguished by structured pilot-to-commercial process + sustained anti-weaponization pledge + ASTM safety-standards leadership.
open_actions_resolved: No new material score-moving evidence; dimensions confirmed
